headline: National Audit Department has 200 unfilled auditor positions
context: The National Audit Department reported 200 unfilled auditor positions out of 1,800 approved posts in 2025, with chronic underfunding contributing to a growing audit backlog.

cards.big: 200 auditor positions unfilled — the agency that watches government spending is undermanned
cards.sub: 11% vacancy rate leaves the national audit function structurally weakened

cards.big: Audit Act 1957 mandates comprehensive audit of all federal expenditure annually
cards.sub: Statutory obligation cannot be met when 11% of audit capacity is missing

cards.big: Each unfilled auditor position represents RM22 million in unaudited government spending
cards.sub: 200 vacancies mean RM4.4 billion in spending receives no audit scrutiny annually

cards.big: Starting salary for government auditors is RM2,800 — private sector offers RM4,500
cards.sub: Pay gap makes recruitment and retention of qualified auditors extremely difficult

cards.big: Underfunding the auditor is the cheapest way to avoid accountability — and governments know it
Should NAD salaries be benchmarked to private sector to attract qualified auditors?
